item.title: Education changes how you think
item.description: My choice has nothing to do with you. You don't have to like my choices or respect them, you just have to not take my choices personally.

item.title: Double wide dump
item.description:
I'm a therapist... So you'll notice a strong theme
My dad used to take me there all the time and this meme was a (mostly good) blast of nostalgia
This one was to send to my brother lol
item.dc:creator: UnpresumptuousUsername

item.title: An uneducated taking advantage of liberal tears and fears
item.dc:creator: Lanhdanan

item.title: So ya, thought ya, might like to go to the show...
item.description: I am an overweight, bald, bearded, 40 something year old white man. Half my shirts advertise car parts and the other half, old rock bands. I have no interest in changing anything, except the overweight part... maybe... one day... I take a lot of pride in being good to people, regardless of race, religion, sex orientation, or gender identity. I've always supported civil rights, women's rights, the right to privacy, and gun control. I work every day to teach my kids to be the same way. Thankfully she defended.
item.dc:creator: downintheparkwithafriendcalledfive

item.title: Birth control is next...
item.description: Source : https://www.nola.com/news/healthcare_hospitals/article_238af184-ff02-11ec-9bce-dfd660a21ce1.html
item.dc:creator: Mech0T1

item.title: Grab a fuckin seat and get ready to do some learnin. We talkin bout physics and shit
item.description: TL;DR: $150 million machine makes computer chips by shining a laser on a light sensitive coating on a silicon wafer FP Edit: I realize A LOT of detail is missing but just wanted to share the absolute basics as well as how fucking cool this machine is. If there's enough interest I'll make a 2nd post going into the nitty gritty details!!

item.title: What if?
item.description: It’s an interesting question regarding States rights and individuals rights. Does Ohio check if it’s citizens imbibed prostitution and cannabis, both illegal in Ohio?

item.dc:creator: hoodbobthugpants

item.title: All in favor of giving Ohio the boot?
item.description: What I find quite interesting is that Ohio is heading back to the 1930's and yet Intel is preparing to make the largest domestic investment in Semiconductor history to set up $80-100 billion worth of computer chip manufacturing facilities. So you have the most advanced tech in one of the least advanced states. If they have any common sense, they'll reconsider and build elsewhere. Sorry, just had to throw that out there as an engineer in the semiconductor industry. P.S. fuck Ohio
item.dc:creator: Longdickjohnson58
